title: Teachable Machine Guide
body:
Guide: Deploying a Google Teachable Machine Model on Hugging Face (Static Space)

Step 1: Prepare Your Files

After exporting your model from Google Teachable Machine, you should have the following structure:

project-folder/ index.html ← your HTML file to load the model tm-my-image-model/ ├── model.json ├── metadata.json └── weights.bin

⚠ The folder must be named exactly: tm-my-image-model

Step 2: Create a New Hugging Face Space

1. Go to https://huggingface.co/spaces 2. Click "Create new Space" 3. Fill in the form: 4. Space name: e.g., Week2 5. License / Description: Optional 6. Select SDK: Choose Static 7. Template: Choose Blank 8. Visibility: Keep as Public 9. Click "Create Space"

Step 3: Upload Your Files Manually

Go to the Files and versions tab in your Space.

Click "+ Add file > Upload file" and upload each file using the exact paths below:

File Path (type this in the upload dialog)

index.html index.html

model.json tm-my-image-model/model.json

metadata.json tm-my-image-model/metadata.json

weights.bin tm-my-image-model/weights.bin

Hugging Face will create the folder tm-my-image-model automatically.

Step 4: Restart the Space

1. Go to the Settings tab 2. Scroll to the bottom and click "Restart Space" 3. Wait 10–20 seconds, then go to the App tab

Step 5: Test Your App

• Click the Start button • Allow camera permissions if prompted • You should now see the webcam and live classification results!

Optional: Clean Up ZIP Files

If you uploaded any .zip files: - Go to Files and versions - Click the trash icon next to the unused ZIPs

Let your students know they can share their working Space link as a demo in presentations or resumes!
